subject: Sociology
title: Peer Culture and Resistance
description: Learn Peer Culture and Resistance in one minute.

hook.text: A smart kid stops raising his hand because his friends call it acting white. Peer culture wins over grades.

buildup.text: Paul Willis studied working-class boys who rejected school, seeing academic effort as a middle-class game.

discovery.text: Their resistance was rational — they saw that credentials didn't guarantee working-class kids real opportunity.

twist.text: But resistance also trapped them. By rejecting school, they ended up in the manual labor they tried to avoid.

climax.text: Peer culture can reinforce inequality even when students see through the system. Resistance doesn't always liberate.

punchline.text: Seeing the trap doesn't free you from it.

quiz.question: What did Paul Willis find about working-class boys and school?
options: They resisted school but ended up in manual labor anyway
